Ramsey Sheibani

Welcome!

My name is Ramsey Sheibani, and I’m a recent mathematics graduate from UC Berkeley interested in pursuing actuarial, analyst, and trading positions. This is my project page, where I will be storing all of my finished projects, as well as writeups for all of them to discuss both my code and my thought processes during development. I will also be including some short snippets of current projects so you can get a sneak peak at what I’ll be creating in the near future. All of these projects are constructed from scratch with no outside assistance. While I do have projects that I completed in university courses, I would consider it academically dishonest to post any of them here, so I will refrain from doing so.

Let me first tell you a little bit about myself.

This is a new sentence to test to local host :)

Portfolio

  • Box Modes Project UC Berkeley

    HFSS simulations of spurious package modes in superconducting chip packages.

  • Ion Milling LBNL

    Developing novel Josephson Junction deposition recipes to improve qubit coherence.

  • Resonator Simulations LBNL

    Developing a simulation framework using the quantum-metal and Ansys HFSS.

  • Superconducting Rectifier UC Berkeley

    Designing a high-frequency superconducting rectifier for cryogenic experiments.

  • Physnotes

    Authoring a comprehensive book on all core undergraduate physics topics.

Blog

Old Projects

  • Whistler Waves SSL

    Data analysis using MATLAB scripts on whistler waves in the solar wind.

  • Coloring the Prokudin Gorskii collection CS 180

    Project 1

  • Fun with Filters and Frequencies CS 180

    Project 2

  • Face Morphing CS 180

    Project 3

  • Image warping, Mosaicing and Autostitching CS 180
